signals.headline: Key details:** Statistics Korea released this data on April 2, 2026, confirming lower-than-expected monthly CPI rise of
signals.raw: Key details:** Statistics Korea released this data on April 2, 2026, confirming lower-than-expected monthly CPI rise of 0.3% (vs. 0.6% forecast) and core inflation at 2.2% YoY (down from 2.3%).[1][2][3] Oil-driven pressures (transport up 5% YoY) were contained by fuel caps and food vouchers, though analysts warn of upside risks from sustained high oil prices (> $100/barrel amid Iran conflict) and
